extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won stage 1 bunch sprint after being boxed behind Laporte, escaped left then centered for huge winning margin
tactical_patterns: Gilbert attacked with teammate already up road showing Quick-Step predetermined two-rider breakaway strategy before peloton organized.
Lawless couldn't hold Gilbert's attack despite making early break, revealing sprinters vulnerable even moderate climbs with 75km remaining.
Late crash 1km out held Lutsenko but Demar/Bennett navigated safely, suggesting positioning behind sprint trains safer than front.
Bennett sprinted on handlebar tops like Tour 2021, claims power advantage over traditional position in final 200m.

rider_signals.LAWLESS Chris: negative: totally dropped on modest climb 75km out despite making early break, lacks transitional terrain resistance
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: at 38 years 246 days (oldest in race) initiated coordinated attack showing Quick-Step still trusts his tactical execution
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: navigated boxed position behind Laporte then won with huge margin using unorthodox tops position sprint
rider_signals.DEMAR Arnaud: positive: safely through late crash and second place suggests superior late-race positioning instincts
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: not in top-10 sprint finish despite bunch arrival, likely crash-affected or poor positioning
